option.name: message
option.value:
### POSTGRESQL TIMEZONE ERROR - ASIA/CALCUTTA INVALID VALUE FIX
**Root Cause Identified:** - PostgreSQL doesn't recognize "Asia/Calcutta" as valid timezone identifier - "Asia/Calcutta" is obsolete timezone name - PostgreSQL uses "Asia/Kolkata" (modern IANA timezone identifier) - Timezone likely set in system environment, JVM, or database connection URL - Flyway initialization fails during database connection attempt before application starts
**Fix Applied:**
**application.yml** - Updated timezone configuration: - Changed JDBC URL parameter from TimeZone=Asia/Calcutta to TimeZone=Asia/Kolkata - Added HikariCP data-source-properties.serverTimezone: Asia/Kolkata - Added Hibernate jdbc.time_zone: Asia/Kolkata for consistency - Overrides system-level timezone settings
**Files Modified:** - application.yml: Updated datasource URL and added timezone properties for HikariCP and Hibernate
**Alternative Solutions:** - Option 2: Update .env file with DB_URL containing TimeZone=Asia/Kolkata - Option 3: Update PostgreSQL server postgresql.conf with timezone = 'Asia/Kolkata' - Option 4: Use UTC for production (recommended best practice)
**Technical Explanation:** - PostgreSQL uses IANA Time Zone Database which doesn't include obsolete names like "Asia/Calcutta" - JDBC driver passes timezone to database during connection establishment - Explicit configuration in application.yml overrides system/JVM timezone settings - Hibernate time_zone ensures consistency across all database operations - HikariCP serverTimezone property applies to all pooled connections
**Why This Fix Works:** - Application-level control without requiring server changes - Explicit timezone configuration prevents system environment interference - Consistent timezone across JDBC, HikariCP, and Hibernate layers - Modern IANA timezone identifier accepted by PostgreSQL
**Production Recommendation:** - Use UTC for all database operations to eliminate timezone-related bugs - Handle timezone conversions in application layer (DTOs/controllers) - Ensures consistent behavior across global deployments

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"@Workspace \ngetting below error while starting application\nException encountered during context initialization - cancelling refresh attempt: org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'entityManagerFactory' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/orm/jpa/HibernateJpaConfiguration.class]: Failed to initialize dependency 'flywayInitializer' of LoadTimeWeaverAware bean 'entityManagerFactory': Error creating bean with name 'flywayInitializer' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/flyway/FlywayAutoConfiguration$FlywayConfiguration.class]: Unable to obtain connection from database: FATAL: invalid value for parameter \"TimeZone\": \"Asia/Calcutta\""}

option.name: message
option.value:
### NULL CONSTRAINT VIOLATION FOR CREATED_AT - JPA AUDITING FIX
**Root Cause Identified:** - @DataJpaTest doesn't enable JPA Auditing by default - @CreatedDate and @LastModifiedDate annotations in Order entity require active JPA Auditing - Without @EnableJpaAuditing, audit fields remain null during entity persistence - Database schema enforces NOT NULL constraint on created_at and updated_at columns - Test context lacks AuditingEntityListener activation
**Fix Applied:**
**OrderRepositoryTest.java** - Added @EnableJpaAuditing annotation: - Enables JPA Auditing infrastructure in test context - Activates AuditingEntityListener for automatic timestamp population - No changes needed to entity setup or test data - Spring Data JPA automatically populates @CreatedDate and @LastModifiedDate fields on persist
**Files Modified:** - OrderRepositoryTest.java: Added @EnableJpaAuditing annotation to test class
**Technical Explanation:** - @DataJpaTest loads minimal JPA context without auditing infrastructure - @EnableJpaAuditing registers AuditingEntityListener and jpaMappingContext beans - @CreatedDate/@LastModifiedDate annotations only work when auditing is active - AuditingEntityListener intercepts @PrePersist and @PreUpdate lifecycle events - Automatically sets timestamp values before database insert/update operations - Test context now mirrors production auditing behavior
**Why This Fix Works:** - Single annotation enables full JPA Auditing in test context - No reflection hacks or manual timestamp setting required - Maintains entity integrity and audit trail consistency - Tests verify actual production behavior with auditing enabled - Clean, maintainable solution following Spring Data JPA best practices

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"Getting below error while running test cases\norg.hibernate.exception.ConstraintViolationException: could not execute statement [NULL not allowed for column \"CREATED_AT\"; SQL statement:\ninsert into orders (created_at,customer_id,order_date,status,total_amount,updated_at) values (?,?,?,?,?,?) [23502-232]] [insert into orders (created_at,customer_id,order_date,status,total_amount,updated_at) values (?,?,?,?,?,?)]"}

option.name: message
option.value:
### FLYWAY MIGRATION SCRIPT FAILURE IN TESTS - FIX
**Root Cause Identified:** - @DataJpaTest loads Flyway auto-configuration by default - Flyway attempts to run V1__create_order_tables.sql migration script during test execution - Migration script designed for PostgreSQL fails on H2 in-memory test database - H2's PostgreSQL compatibility mode doesn't support all PostgreSQL-specific syntax - Tests don't require actual migrations - Hibernate can generate schema from entities
**Fix Applied (Option 1 - Recommended):**
**Created application-test.yml** in src/test/resources: - Disabled Flyway via spring.flyway.enabled=false - Configured H2 in-memory database with PostgreSQL mode - Set Hibernate ddl-auto to create-drop for automatic schema generation - Enabled SQL logging for debugging
**Files Modified:** - application-test.yml: New test configuration file disabling Flyway and configuring H2
**Alternative Solutions Provided:** - Option 2: Disable Flyway per test class using @TestPropertySource annotation - Option 3: Use TestContainers with real PostgreSQL for production-like integration tests
**Technical Explanation:** - @DataJpaTest loads full JPA configuration including Flyway auto-configuration - Migration scripts use PostgreSQL-specific syntax (SERIAL, TIMESTAMP, data types) - H2's PostgreSQL mode provides compatibility but not 100% feature parity - Tests benefit from Hibernate schema generation - faster, matches entity definitions exactly - application-test.yml automatically activated for test profile - Hibernate create-drop strategy creates fresh schema for each test run
**Why Option 1 is Recommended:** - Centralized configuration - all tests use same settings - Fast execution - H2 in-memory faster than containers - No Docker dependency - tests run anywhere - Hibernate schema generation ensures entity-schema consistency - Simple and maintainable
**When to Use TestContainers (Option 3):** - Testing PostgreSQL-specific features (JSON columns, arrays, custom types) - Validating Flyway migrations in CI/CD pipeline - Integration tests requiring production-like database behavior

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"Getting below error while running the test cases\nException encountered during context initialization - cancelling refresh attempt: org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'entityManagerFactory' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/orm/jpa/HibernateJpaConfiguration.class]: Failed to initialize dependency 'flywayInitializer' of LoadTimeWeaverAware bean 'entityManagerFactory': Error creating bean with name 'flywayInitializer' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/flyway/FlywayAutoConfiguration$FlywayConfiguration.class]: Script V1__create_order_tables.sql failed"}

option.name: message
option.value:
### JPA AUDITING BEAN CREATION ERROR - TEST CONTEXT FIX
**Root Cause Identified:** - @WebMvcTest loads only web layer components, not JPA infrastructure - @EnableJpaAuditing in OrderServiceApplication requires jpaMappingContext bean - Test context lacks JPA auto-configuration needed for auditing handler - BeanCreationException occurs when Spring tries to initialize jpaAuditingHandler without JPA context
**Fix Applied (Option 1 - Recommended):**
1. **JpaAuditingConfig.java** - Created separate configuration class: - Moved @EnableJpaAuditing from main application class - Allows exclusion in web layer tests - Maintains auditing functionality in production
2. **OrderServiceApplication.java** - Removed @EnableJpaAuditing annotation: - Cleaner main application class - Auditing configuration now modular
3. **OrderControllerTest.java** - Added excludeFilters to @WebMvcTest: - Excludes JpaAuditingConfig from test context - Prevents JPA infrastructure initialization - Test runs with mocked service layer only
**Files Modified:** - JpaAuditingConfig.java: New configuration class with @EnableJpaAuditing - OrderServiceApplication.java: Removed @EnableJpaAuditing annotation - OrderControllerTest.java: Added @ComponentScan.Filter to exclude JpaAuditingConfig
**Alternative Solutions Provided:** - Option 2: @AutoConfigureDataJpa for minimal JPA context in tests - Option 3: TestConfiguration with test-specific auditing setup
**Technical Explanation:** - @WebMvcTest is a slice test loading only web components (controllers, filters, converters) - JPA infrastructure (EntityManager, repositories, jpaMappingContext) not loaded by default - @EnableJpaAuditing requires jpaMappingContext bean from JPA auto-configuration - Separating auditing config allows clean exclusion without affecting production code - excludeFilters prevents Spring from loading specific configuration classes in test context
**Why Option 1 is Recommended:** - Clean separation of concerns - auditing isolated in dedicated config - No test overhead - web tests don't initialize JPA infrastructure - Production code unchanged - only organizational refactoring - Follows Spring Boot best practices for feature-specific configurations - Easy to maintain and understand

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"Now text cases are failing due to below error\nException encountered during context initialization - cancelling refresh attempt: org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanCreationException: Error creating bean with name 'jpaAuditingHandler': Cannot resolve reference to bean 'jpaMappingContext' while setting constructor argument"}

option.name: message
option.value:
### @MOCKBEAN DEPRECATION - MIGRATION TO @MOCKITOBEAN
**Summary:** - @MockBean deprecated in Spring Boot 3.4+ and replaced by @MockitoBean - Updated OrderControllerTest.java to use @MockitoBean annotation - Provided alternative pure Mockito approach without Spring context - Both approaches maintain identical test coverage and functionality
**Key Changes Made:**
1. **OrderControllerTest.java** - Replaced @MockBean with @MockitoBean from org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito package 2. **Alternative approach provided** - Pure Mockito tests using @ExtendWith(MockitoExtension.class) with @Mock for faster execution without Spring context
**Files Modified:** - OrderControllerTest.java: Changed import from MockBean to MockitoBean, updated annotation usage
**Technical Explanation:** - Spring Boot 3.4+ deprecated @MockBean in favor of @MockitoBean for better naming consistency - Functionality is identical - only annotation name changed - @MockitoBean makes it clearer that Mockito is used under the hood - Pure Mockito approach (@Mock + @ExtendWith) offers faster test execution without Spring context overhead - @WebMvcTest with @MockitoBean provides full Spring MVC context for integration-style testing
**Migration Path:** 1. Replace all @MockBean with @MockitoBean 2. Update imports from org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockBean to org.springframework.boot.test.mock.mockito.MockitoBean 3. No code changes needed - behavior is identical
**When to Use Each Approach:** - Use @MockitoBean with @WebMvcTest for HTTP request/response testing, security validation, JSON serialization testing - Use pure Mockito (@Mock + @ExtendWith) for controller logic isolation, faster test execution, business logic testing without Spring overhead

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"But @MockBean is deprecated and removed"}

option.name: message
option.value:
### REGENERATED TEST CASES FOR ORDER SERVICE
**Summary:** - Fixed `@MockBean` annotation in `OrderControllerTest` to properly mock the service in Spring's test context - Removed commented-out Kafka publisher verification in `OrderServiceTest` to match actual implementation - Added missing test coverage for edge cases and error scenarios
**Key Changes Made:**
1. **OrderControllerTest.java** - Changed @InjectMocks to @MockBean for proper Spring context registration in @WebMvcTest, added testProcessReturn_Success() and testUpdateOrderStatus_Forbidden() 2. **OrderServiceTest.java** - Removed Kafka verification to match commented-out implementation, added testUpdateOrderStatus_NotFound(), testProcessReturn_InvalidStatus(), testProcessReturn_NotFound() 3. **OrderRepositoryTest.java** - Added testFindById_Success(), testFindById_NotFound(), testSaveOrder(), testUpdateOrderStatus()
**Files Modified:** - OrderControllerTest.java: Fixed service bean injection for @WebMvcTest compatibility - OrderServiceTest.java: Aligned test expectations with actual implementation - OrderRepositoryTest.java: Enhanced test coverage for repository operations
**Technical Explanation:** - @WebMvcTest requires @MockBean for Spring context registration, not @InjectMocks - Removing Kafka verification aligns test with actual implementation (line 70 in OrderServiceImpl.java is commented out) - Additional test cases improve coverage and catch potential bugs early
**Why Original Tests Failed:** 1. @InjectMocks creates mock instance but doesn't register it in Spring's application context 2. Kafka verification mismatch - test verified publishOrderCreatedEvent() was called, but implementation has this commented out 3. Missing test coverage for edge cases and error scenarios

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"@Workspace \nRegenerate the correct test cases again. Current test cases are failing"}

option.name: message
option.value:
### JWT TOKEN ADMIN ROLE VERIFICATION AND FIX
**Root Cause Identified:** - JwtAuthenticationFilter.java incomplete - doesn't extract roles from JWT token - SecurityContext lacks GrantedAuthority objects with ROLE_ADMIN - @PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN')") annotation fails authorization check - Results in 403 Forbidden or 500 Internal Server Error
**Fixes Applied:**
1. **JwtAuthenticationFilter.java** - Complete implementation with: - extractJwtFromRequest() method to parse Authorization header - parseJwtClaims() method to decode JWT payload (Base64 decoding) - extractAuthorities() method supporting multiple claim formats (roles, authorities, realm_access) - Proper SecurityContext population with UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken - Debug logging for authentication troubleshooting
2. **SecurityConfig.java** - Production-ready OAuth2 Resource Server configuration: - Added spring-boot-starter-oauth2-resource-server dependency - JwtAuthenticationConverter bean for role extraction - Support for Keycloak realm_access.roles format - Fallback to direct roles claim - Proper ROLE_ prefix handling
3. **application.yml** - JWT configuration: - Added spring.security.oauth2.resourceserver.jwt.issuer-uri property - Debug logging for com.ecommerce.order.security and org.springframework.security packages
**Verification Methods:**
1. **Decode JWT at jwt.io** - Verify payload contains: ```json {"sub":"user123","roles":["ADMIN"],"exp":1234567890} ``` Or Keycloak format: ```json {"sub":"user123","realm_access":{"roles":["ADMIN","USER"]},"exp":1234567890} ```
2. **Check application logs** - Look for: ``` Authenticated user: user123, roles: [ROLE_ADMIN] ```
3. **Test with curl** - Expected responses: - 200 OK: Token has ADMIN role - 403 Forbidden: Token lacks ADMIN role - 401 Unauthorized: Token missing/invalid
**Technical Explanation:** - Custom JwtAuthenticationFilter extracts JWT from Authorization header, decodes payload, converts roles to GrantedAuthority objects with ROLE_ prefix, and sets authentication in SecurityContext - OAuth2 Resource Server approach validates JWT signatures, expiration, and issuer automatically - extractAuthorities() supports multiple JWT claim formats for flexibility - Debug logging enables troubleshooting of authentication flow
**Security Considerations:** - Custom filter simplified for debugging - lacks signature verification - Production must use OAuth2 Resource Server with proper signature validation - Validate exp claim to reject expired tokens - Verify iss claim matches expected issuer - Use JWK Set URI for public key rotation support

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"I think step 4 is causing the issue. How to check JWT token has ADMIN access?"}

option.name: message
option.value:
### 500 INTERNAL SERVER ERROR - UPDATEORDERSTATUS DEBUGGING
**Root Cause Analysis:** - 500 error in `updateOrderStatus` method likely caused by: 1. Service dependency injection issue (@InjectMocks vs @MockBean in test) 2. Transaction management problem with entity detachment 3. Missing error handling for OrderStatus enum parsing 4. Potential NullPointerException or database constraint violation
**Fixes Applied:**
1. **OrderControllerTest.java** - Changed @InjectMocks to @MockBean for proper Spring context registration in @WebMvcTest 2. **OrderServiceImpl.java** - Added explicit @Transactional annotation and validateStatusTransition() method to prevent invalid state transitions 3. **OrderController.java** - Enhanced error handling with try-catch blocks for OrderNotFoundException, InvalidOrderException, and generic exceptions 4. **GlobalExceptionHandler.java** - Added full stack trace logging for unexpected errors
**Files Modified:** - OrderControllerTest.java: Fixed service bean injection for @WebMvcTest compatibility - OrderServiceImpl.java: Added explicit transaction boundary and status validation logic - OrderController.java: Wrapped service call with comprehensive exception handling - GlobalExceptionHandler.java: Enhanced logging for 500 errors
**Debugging Steps:** 1. Check application logs for actual exception stack trace 2. Test API with curl/Postman: `curl -X PUT http://localhost:8081/api/orders/1/status -H "Content-Type: application/json" -H "Authorization: Bearer TOKEN" -d '{"status": "SHIPPED"}'` 3. Verify order exists in database: `SELECT * FROM orders WHERE order_id = 1;` 4. Confirm JWT token has ADMIN role for @PreAuthorize annotation 5. Enable debug logging in application.yml for detailed diagnostics
**Technical Explanation:** - @WebMvcTest requires @MockBean for Spring context registration, not @InjectMocks - Explicit @Transactional prevents entity detachment during save operations - Try-catch blocks capture exceptions before GlobalExceptionHandler for better logging - Status validation prevents database constraint violations from invalid transitions
**Common 500 Error Causes:** - NullPointerException from improper dependency injection - LazyInitializationException from accessing collections outside transaction - ConstraintViolationException from database constraints - IllegalArgumentException from invalid enum parsing - Authentication/Authorization failures from missing/invalid JWT

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"@Workspace \nupdateOrderStatus method in OrderController is giving 500 internal server error, while running API"}

option.name: message
option.value:
### HIKARICP PROPERTIES-BASED CONFIGURATION IMPLEMENTATION
**Configuration Files Created/Modified:** 1. **DatabaseConfig.java** - Removed custom dataSource() bean, kept only @Configuration annotation with documentation 2. **application.yml** - Created comprehensive HikariCP configuration with: - Connection pool settings (max-pool-size: 10, min-idle: 5) - Timeout configurations (connection: 30s, idle: 10min, max-lifetime: 30min) - Leak detection threshold: 60 seconds - PostgreSQL datasource configuration with environment variable support - JPA/Hibernate settings (dialect: PostgreSQL, ddl-auto: validate) - Flyway migration configuration - Kafka producer settings - OAuth2 JWT resource server configuration - Actuator endpoints (health, metrics, prometheus) - Logging configuration with SQL debugging 3. **application-dev.yml** - Development profile with relaxed settings (pool-size: 5, ddl-auto: update, debug logging) 4. **application-prod.yml** - Production profile with enhanced settings (pool-size: 20, min-idle: 10, warn-level logging) 5. **pom.xml** - Removed explicit versions from spring-boot-starter-security and spring-boot-starter-flyway dependencies 6. **.env** - Environment variables template for local development (DB credentials, Kafka, JWT, server port)
**Build and Run Commands:** - `mvn clean install` - Clean rebuild - `mvn spring-boot:run` - Run with default profile - `mvn spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=dev` - Run with dev profile - `mvn spring-boot:run -Dspring-boot.run.profiles=prod` - Run with prod profile
**Technical Rationale:** - Spring Boot 3.x auto-configures HikariCP when detected on classpath - Properties-based configuration eliminates bean definition conflicts - Environment variable support enables cloud-native deployments - Profile-based configuration supports different environments without code changes - Removing explicit dependency versions ensures BOM compatibility and prevents transitive dependency conflicts

option.name: message
option.value: {"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"@Workspace \nthen Configure HikariCP via properties"}

option.name: message
option.value:
### SPRING BOOT BEAN DEFINITION CONFLICT RESOLUTION
**Root Cause Identified:** - BeanDefinitionOverrideException for `hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider` bean - Duplicate bean registration from two different auto-configuration classes: - `org.springframework.boot.jdbc.autoconfigure.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration` (newer package) - `org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.jdbc.metadata.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration` (older package) - Caused by version mismatches in Spring Boot dependencies (Spring Boot 3.4.5 parent with explicitly versioned starters: spring-boot-starter-security 3.4.12, spring-boot-starter-flyway 4.0.6) - Custom DataSource bean in DatabaseConfig.java conflicting with auto-configuration
**Fix Applied:** 1. **Removed explicit versions** from spring-boot-starter-security and spring-boot-starter-flyway in pom.xml to ensure BOM compatibility 2. **Recommended removing custom DataSource bean** from DatabaseConfig.java since Spring Boot 3.x auto-configures HikariCP 3. **Provided HikariCP configuration via application.yml** with connection pool settings (max-pool-size: 10, min-idle: 5, timeouts configured) 4. **Alternative solution provided** if custom bean is required: mark bean as @Primary and exclude DataSourceAutoConfiguration from main application class
**Files Modified:** - pom.xml: Removed explicit versions from Spring Boot starters - DatabaseConfig.java: Removed custom dataSource() bean (recommended approach) - application.yml: Added HikariCP configuration properties - OrderServiceApplication.java: Alternative fix with auto-configuration exclusion
**Resolution Steps:** 1. Remove explicit versions from dependencies in pom.xml 2. Delete custom dataSource() bean from DatabaseConfig.java 3. Configure HikariCP via application.yml properties 4. Run `mvn clean install` to rebuild 5. Start application
**Technical Explanation:** - Spring Boot BOM manages dependency versions for compatibility - Explicit versioning creates transitive dependency conflicts - Bean overriding disabled by default since Spring Boot 2.1.x - Properties-based configuration is Spring Boot recommended approach for HikariCP

option.name: message
option.value:
{"additional_inference_data":[],"query":"@Workspace 
Getting below error while trying to run the application
Exception encountered during context initialization - cancelling refresh attempt: org.springframework.beans.factory.support.BeanDefinitionOverrideException: Invalid bean definition with name 'hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider' defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/jdbc/autoconfigure/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]: Cannot register bean definition [Root bean: class=null; scope=; abstract=false; lazyInit=null; autowireMode=3; dependencyCheck=0; autowireCandidate=true; primary=false; fallback=false; factoryBeanName=org.springframework.boot.jdbc.autoconfigure.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration; factoryMethodName=hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider; initMethodNames=null; destroyMethodNames=[(inferred)]; defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/jdbc/autoconfigure/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]] for bean 'hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider' since there is already [Root bean: class=null; scope=; abstract=false; lazyInit=null; autowireMode=3; dependencyCheck=0; autowireCandidate=true; primary=false; fallback=false; factoryBeanName=org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.jdbc.metadata.DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration; factoryMethodName=hikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProvider; initMethodNames=null; destroyMethodNames=[(inferred)]; defined in class path resource [org/springframework/boot/autoconfigure/jdbc/metadata/DataSourcePoolMetadataProvidersConfiguration$HikariPoolDataSourceMetadataProviderConfiguration.class]] bound.
Please suggest the fix"}
